violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2026

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. PIC contacted service at time of inspection. Establishment to sanitize in 3 bay sink until serviced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (dry storage containers) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2026

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the basement area. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2026

Wood is used in the construction of beams in the ware washing area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2026

The floor and wall junctures in the ware washing area is in poor repair, missing/chipped pieces. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (lime juice) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. PIC fixed with booster at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

Marinara sauce was observed held at 113 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

Various foods were observed held at 54-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. See disposal. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

Deli meat was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout kitchen. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the basement area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

Wood is used in the construction of beams in the ware washing area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026
Prep unit #1 was not in good repair, ambient observed at 58F. PIC took unit out of service at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. 1. Shelving throughout kitchen (food residue/debris) 2. Ceiling tile above ice machine (previous water damage) 3. Refrigeration (food residue/debris) 4. Utensil storage containers (food residue/debris) 5. Refrigerator #1 compressor compartment (rodent droppings) New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The floor and wall junctures in the ware washing area is in poor repair, missing/chipped pieces. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2026

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety manager course. Per person in charge, employees are scheduled to take the course this Thursday. Please email inspector proof of completion of course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety manager course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety manager course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as deli meat) in the refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as deli meat during storage. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Dishwasher was not heated up before use. Establishment must make sure dishwasher is heated up before using it. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

Establishment is using time as a public health control for pizza slices. Establishment could not provide inspector with time log records at time of inspection. Establishment must fill out time logs. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken was placed hot in the prep unit in containers greater than four inches deep. Per person in charge, chicken was cooked about 1.5 hours prior to inspection. The person in charge relocated the chicken to a shallow pan and placed it in the walk-in cooler to rapidly cool it. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

The cutting board of the prep unit are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety manager course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety course. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (carrots) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Carrots were voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as deli meat) in the refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as deli meat during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Service technician fixed the dishwasher at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was cleaned at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

Establishment is using time as a public health control for pizza slices without prior approval. An application and time log were given to the person in charge at time of inspection. Establishment must submit an application and fill out time logs. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Chicken was relocated to a shallow pan to cool. Cooling methods were reviewed with the person in charge at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

Hamburgers and shaved steak were observed thawing on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

The cutting board of the prep unit is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the sanitizing spray bottle. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023
The insect control device (light trap) was observed installed over equipment. Insect control device must be relocated. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023

The strainer located in the ware-washing area was not in good repair. Metal wires were broken. The person in charge took the strainer out of service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigeration units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/28/2023
